hallucinations.description: The paper cites two 2025 papers (PiKE by Li et al. and AutoMixAlign by Corrado et al.) that do not exist in the real literature and appear fabricated for the narrative.
hallucinations.evidence: References section lists 'Li et al. (2025)' and 'Corrado et al. (2025)' with arXiv-like identifiers; no such papers exist.

hallucinations.description: The experimental setup claims a 128M-parameter model with only 3 layers and 4 heads, which is mathematically impossible (actual parameter count is ~1M).
hallucinations.evidence: Section 4: 'transformer models (128M parameters, 3 layers, 4 attention heads)'; code confirms d_model=128, 3 layers, vocab=500 yields far fewer parameters.

hallucinations.description: Specific quantitative claims (5.2% validation loss degradation, 4.4% std improvement, exact loss values 3.566 vs 3.389) do not match any run in the provided experimental data or code outputs.
hallucinations.evidence: Abstract and Section 5 report precise metrics; ablation JSONs and plot analyses show different loss/balance values and often show static mixing performing similarly or better.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found: nonexistent citations, inconsistent model size claims, and fabricated experimental numbers that contradict the actual code/results. Severity is moderate-high as core claims are unsupported.
